_note: 50 additional real, web-researched Israel programs gathered 2026-07-07, spanning universities, tech/business internships, volunteer/service, pre-army mechinot, Hebrew ulpan, and yeshivot/seminaries. Cross-checked by name against all 105 programs already in the database before writing to avoid duplicates. Where an organization runs multiple distinct named tracks (e.g. World Bnei Akiva's Kadima/Mechina Olamit/Torani/MTA, or Neve Yerushalayim's Shalhevet/Machon Devorah divisions), each track is its own entry, matching the precedent already set by the existing BBYO Passport: Israel Journey / Israel Trek pair. durationType is one of TEN_DAY|SUMMER|SEMESTER|GAP_YEAR|CUSTOM. A handful of fields (contactEmail/contactPhone/cost) are left as empty strings where no public figure could be verified, rather than guessed.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Ben-Gurion University – Ginsburg-Ingerman Overseas Student Program (OSP)
universities_and_academic_programs.description: A semester or year-long study abroad program at Ben-Gurion University taught in English, covering fields from anthropology and political science to environmental studies and global health, preceded by a six-week Hebrew ulpan immersion course before the semester begins.
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply online through the BGU International / Overseas Student Program office; a separate Israeli student visa application is required after acceptance.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Ariel University International Program
universities_and_academic_programs.description: An English-language study track for overseas students combining first-semester courses from Ariel's three-year undergraduate programs with an intensive 500-hour Hebrew ulpan, run in partnership with Masa Israel Journey; students live and study alongside Israeli peers on the Ariel campus.
universities_and_academic_programs.durationText: 1 semester up to a full year
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through Ariel University's international admissions office or via a Masa Israel Journey program listing; a student visa and proof of health insurance are required after acceptance.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Sapir Academic College International Program
universities_and_academic_programs.description: Semester-based English-language courses for international students in fields including marketing, communications, cinema, law, economics and social work at Israel's largest public college in the Gaza-envelope region, alongside global partner-institution exchange tracks and a preparatory Mechina for students who need it.
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through Sapir College's International Office; visa, health insurance and proof of financial means are required for international students.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Jerusalem Academy of Music and Dance – DanceJerusalem
universities_and_academic_programs.organization: Jerusalem Academy of Music and Dance, in partnership with Hebrew University's Rothberg International School
universities_and_academic_programs.description: A university-level dance program for international students combining technical training in classical ballet, contemporary dance (including Ohad Naharin's Gaga), choreography and improvisation with Rothberg International School's academic coursework on Israeli history, society and culture.
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply jointly through the Rothberg International School and JAMD admissions offices; an audition/portfolio is required. A student (A/2) visa, acceptance letter and proof of health insurance are needed.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Weizmann Institute – Kupcinet-Getz International Summer School
universities_and_academic_programs.description: A highly competitive 7-week summer research program (running since 1971) placing outstanding undergraduates from physical sciences, chemistry, life sciences, math or computer science majors into a Weizmann lab under a research scientist's supervision, working on a real research project alongside the scientific team.
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply online with a CV, transcript, letters of recommendation and a letter of motivation; a 3.6/4.0 GPA equivalent and fluency in English are required. Competitive, selective admissions.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Musrara International Residency Program
universities_and_academic_programs.organization: Musrara – The Naggar School of Art and Society
universities_and_academic_programs.description: A juried two-week international artist residency at Musrara's Naggar School selecting five interdisciplinary artists from Israel and abroad working in photography, new media, video art, sound and experimental music, alongside the school's ongoing three-year photography, new-media and phototherapy departments.
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply with a portfolio/project proposal through Musrara's international programs page; selection is competitive.

universities_and_academic_programs.name: Sam Spiegel Jerusalem International Film Lab
universities_and_academic_programs.organization: Sam Spiegel Film and Television School
universities_and_academic_programs.description: An internationally-ranked film school's lab program bringing a small cohort of outstanding local and international emerging filmmakers to Jerusalem to develop their debut feature film projects, alongside the school's separate four-year directing/producing and two-year screenwriting degree tracks.
universities_and_academic_programs.durationText: Multi-session lab spread across roughly a year; degree tracks are 2-4 years
universities_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply with a feature film project/script through the Jerusalem International Film Lab's call for submissions; highly selective (12 filmmakers per cohort).

tech_and_business_internships.name: Birthright Israel Excel Fellowship
tech_and_business_internships.description: A highly selective, lifelong business and tech fellowship for outstanding Jewish college students that begins with a 10-week summer internship in Tel Aviv at a Fortune 500 company, top consulting firm or Israeli startup across fields like finance, venture capital, software engineering and biotech, followed by ongoing mentorship and a 1,500+ alumni professional network.
tech_and_business_internships.durationText: 10-week summer internship, plus multi-year fellowship community
tech_and_business_internships.signupInstructions: Apply online during the fall/winter application cycle; minimum 3.2 GPA (or equivalent), demonstrated leadership and eligibility under Birthright's Jewish-heritage criteria are required. Highly competitive.

tech_and_business_internships.name: Tech2Peace
tech_and_business_internships.location: Jerusalem area and West Bank sites (Al Quds, Abu Dis, Jericho)
tech_and_business_internships.description: An intensive 10-day seminar bringing together Israelis and Palestinians ages 20-30 for 18 hours of hands-on tech/entrepreneurship training alongside 18 hours of facilitated conflict-dialogue sessions and a hackathon, followed by ongoing alumni dialogue and cultural-exchange programming.
tech_and_business_internships.signupInstructions: Apply online via the Tech2Peace application page; highly competitive (roughly 500 applicants for 60 spots per cohort).

tech_and_business_internships.name: Big Idea Gap Year
tech_and_business_internships.description: Israel's only hi-tech-focused gap year, combining a 4-month coding bootcamp (earning a Front-End Developer certificate in mobile and web app development) with a hands-on internship at Be'er Sheva tech companies and start-ups such as Wix and PayPal, plus Hebrew study and organized trips around Israel.
tech_and_business_internships.signupInstructions: Apply through the Big Idea / Aardvark Israel or Masa Israel program pages; a Masa Israel grant may reduce the cost for eligible participants.

tech_and_business_internships.name: Torah Tech
tech_and_business_internships.organization: Torah Tech (an Am Yisrael Foundation initiative)
tech_and_business_internships.description: An elite gap-year program for young men combining a Rosh Yeshiva-led Beit Midrash focused on Torah study and business ethics with real internships at Tel Aviv startups, aiming to prepare talmidim for both a Torah-centered life and a career in the Startup Nation's tech industry.
tech_and_business_internships.signupInstructions: Apply through torahtech.co or the Masa Israel program listing for Masa Torah Tech.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Project TEN (Kibbutz Harduf)
volunteer_and_service_programs.organization: Project TEN / The Jewish Agency for Israel
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: A service-learning center co-directed by a Jewish woman and a Muslim man where young-adult volunteers weekly support Beit Elisha (a group home for adults with disabilities) and Sha'ar La'adam's ecological/educational activities, alongside pluralistic Jewish learning, Hebrew and Arabic classes, coexistence sessions, theater and bio-dynamic gardening.
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Apply via the Jewish Agency's Project TEN program page; Masa grants may be available for eligible participants.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Save a Child's Heart Volunteer Program
volunteer_and_service_programs.location: Holon (Wolfson Medical Center / Sylvan Adams Children's Hospital)
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: Volunteers support children from developing countries receiving pediatric cardiac care, organizing games and activities and providing companionship and emotional support to patients and families before and after their procedures; tracks range from an 'Immersive' 3-week minimum to longer 'Superstar' and 'Buddy' commitments of 2+ months.
volunteer_and_service_programs.cost: Free to volunteer; participants arrange and cover their own accommodations
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Complete the volunteer application Google Form on the Save a Child's Heart website; minimum age 18.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Leket Israel Volunteer Program
volunteer_and_service_programs.organization: Leket Israel — The National Food Bank
volunteer_and_service_programs.location: Nationwide (fields, packing centers and partner NPOs across Israel)
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: Israel's national food-rescue organization runs gleaning trips to pick surplus produce in the fields, night-time food rescue runs, and sandwich-packing sessions for schoolchildren, redistributing rescued food to a network of 200+ partner nonprofits serving over 175,000 people weekly; sessions require no prior training or professional credentials.
volunteer_and_service_programs.durationText: Flexible — single sessions up to ongoing/recurring volunteering
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Contact Leket Israel directly to schedule a volunteer session or gleaning trip; group and individual placements available.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Hadassah Medical Center Volunteer Program
volunteer_and_service_programs.location: Jerusalem (Ein Kerem and Mount Scopus campuses)
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: A Foreign Volunteer Program placing short-term overseas volunteers alongside nursing staff, in service laboratories, and in patient-comfort and care roles at Hadassah's two university hospitals, with a coordinator introducing each volunteer to their unit and staying in touch throughout their placement.
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Write directly to the volunteer program coordinator to arrange a placement.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Lachish
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Judean foothills — campuses at Nahal Oz, Eliav and Kibbutz Beit Guvrin
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military gap-year academy combining intensive study of Jewish identity, Zionism and Israeli society with coursework in the arts and environmentalism, plus independent study, community volunteering and physical preparation for IDF service.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Telem
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy set in a dense urban environment, emphasizing social justice and coexistence work within Tel Aviv-Jaffa's diverse Jewish and Arab communities alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat HaEmek
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Kfar Rupin Kibbutz, near the Sea of Galilee
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy on a kibbutz near the Sea of Galilee emphasizing nature exploration and building bridges across different sectors of Israeli society, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Meitzar
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy on the Golan Heights focused on cultivating initiative, responsibility and leadership toward building a democratic Jewish society, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity study and volunteering.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Five Fingers (Chamesh Etzbaot)
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy established in 2018 that uses a distinctive physical-and-mental training system to build modern-age resilience and life skills, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Melach HaAretz
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Dead Sea region — Ein Gedi (girls) and Mitzpe Shalem (boys)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, gender-separated pre-military academy in the Dead Sea region combining the standard mechina curriculum of Jewish identity, Zionism and leadership with the unique desert and Dead Sea environment across its two single-gender campuses.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Aderet
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Ayanot, Ben Shemen and Kfar Silver
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy across three campuses focused on cultivating a new generation of Israeli leadership with an emphasis on social and environmental responsibility, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Amichai
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy in Beer Sheva open to students of all religious backgrounds, combining standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study with community volunteering in the Negev.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Hannaton
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy in the Yizrael Valley centered on exploring Jewish-Israeli identity through study, leadership training and volunteer community work.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Rakia
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military leadership academy in Harish drawing on Israeli Air Force heritage and values as a framework for its leadership training, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Arava
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Hatzeva, Paran, Zofar and Ein-Yahav (Arava Valley)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy across several small Arava Valley communities, deliberately open to all Jewish denominations to maximize the diversity of its cohort, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Be'eri
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy emphasizing progressive and Zionist leadership development, alongside standard mechina Jewish-identity study and community volunteering.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Dror
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A secular/pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy in the western Negev near the Gaza border, combining standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study with community volunteering in the region.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Tavor
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.location: Northern Israel — Nof HaGalil, Ramat HaShofet and Kiryat Shmona
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy spanning several northern Israel communities, suitable for students of any religious background, combining standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study with community volunteering.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechinat Haviva Reich
pre_army_mechinot.organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A pluralistic, co-ed pre-military academy named for Haviva Reich, one of the WWII Jewish parachutists, combining standard mechina Jewish-identity and leadership study with community volunteering and physical preparation for IDF service.
pre_army_mechinot.cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Register through the Yachad Gap Year program at mechinot.org.il, attend a fall informative meeting, rank preferred mechinot, interview, and attend the mandatory spring prep seminar and August orientation in Israel.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Kadima (World Bnei Akiva)
pre_army_mechinot.description: An immersive, co-ed, modern-Orthodox Zionist gap-year program giving participants a broad taste of Israeli life through internships, kibbutz living, volunteering, Magen David Adom training, a taste of Marva IDF preparation, seminars, Torah learning and travel.
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply through worldbneiakiva.org's gap year program page.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Mechina Olamit (World Bnei Akiva)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A 9.5-month, co-educational leadership gap year combining shared living with Israeli peers, immersive Zionist learning and hands-on leadership experiences at World Bnei Akiva's flagship international mechina campus.
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply through worldbneiakiva.org's Mechina Olamit program page.

pre_army_mechinot.name: Torani (World Bnei Akiva)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A World Bnei Akiva gap-year track designed to enhance a participant's parallel year in yeshiva or seminary with additional leadership training, seminars, Shabbatonim, a kibbutz stay and a wider Israel-wide peer community.
pre_army_mechinot.durationText: Full gap year, run alongside a yeshiva/seminary program
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply through worldbneiakiva.org's Torani program page; typically taken alongside enrollment in a separate yeshiva or midrasha.

pre_army_mechinot.name: MTA (World Bnei Akiva)
pre_army_mechinot.description: A World Bnei Akiva year program geared specifically toward participants from Australia, New Zealand and South Africa, immersing them in Beit Midrash learning and Torat Eretz Yisrael at one of four campuses.
pre_army_mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply through worldbneiakiva.org's MTA program page; intended primarily for Australian, New Zealand and South African participants.

hebrew_ulpan.name: Ulpan Etzion
hebrew_ulpan.location: Jerusalem, Haifa, Ra'anana, Ramle, Be'er Sheva and Kibbutz Tzuba
hebrew_ulpan.description: The Jewish Agency's flagship Hebrew ulpan, founded in 1949, offering five months of intensive Hebrew instruction (5 hours/day, 5 days/week) to roughly 1,600 immigrants and young adults aged 22-35 per year from 30+ countries, with cultural activities and preparation for IDF service or Israeli employment.
hebrew_ulpan.durationText: 5 months (an optional 3-month Etzion Plus extension is available at the Ra'anana campus)
hebrew_ulpan.signupInstructions: Apply through the Jewish Agency's Ulpan Etzion program page; primarily intended for young-adult olim and prospective immigrants holding a college degree.

hebrew_ulpan.name: Kibbutz Ulpan
hebrew_ulpan.location: Ein Hashofet, Ma'agan Michael, Sde Eliyahu and Etzion Tzuba kibbutzim
hebrew_ulpan.description: A 5-month program combining multi-level Hebrew language classes with kibbutz dormitory living and work assignments, giving participants an immersive experience of rural Israeli community life alongside their Hebrew studies.
hebrew_ulpan.signupInstructions: Apply through kibbutzulpan.org's 'how to apply' page; Masa Israel grants may be available for eligible participants. Intended for tourists and prospective immigrants (existing olim should apply via the Jewish Agency instead).

hebrew_ulpan.name: Ulpan-Or Sabra Immersion Program
hebrew_ulpan.description: A personalized, one-on-one Hebrew program built around the 'Rapid Language Acquisition' method, with the flagship Sabra Immersion track combining three hours of daily one-on-one study with 1.5 hours of interactive outdoor activities; also offered as 10-day, one-week and two-week intensives.
hebrew_ulpan.durationText: 10 days to several weeks, fully personalized
hebrew_ulpan.signupInstructions: Contact Ulpan-Or through their website to schedule a level assessment and build a personalized study plan.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Neve Yerushalayim – Shalhevet Program
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A one-year program at the world's oldest and largest college for Jewish women (35,000+ alumni since 1970), designed for women who want to deepen their Jewish knowledge, learning skills and spiritual growth in an environment built specifically for baalot teshuva.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Neve Yerushalayim's admissions office or via the Masa Israel program listing for Neve Yerushalayim General Jewish Studies.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Neve Yerushalayim – Machon Devorah
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: One of Neve Yerushalayim's eleven affiliated schools on its Har Nof campus, offering a dedicated seminary track for post-high-school and graduate-age women alongside the flagship Shalhevet program, part of the broader Neve network founded in 1970.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Neve Yerushalayim's admissions office.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Ohr Somayach – Derech Institute for Torah Studies
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A program at the Jerusalem baal-teshuva yeshiva founded in 1970, designed for motivated post-high-school students who may lack a strong formal Jewish education but have the intellectual drive to independently study classical Jewish texts including Talmud and responsa, taught by faculty trained to teach from absolute zero.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.durationText: 1 year (customized programs also available for South African and Australian post-high-school students)
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Ohr Somayach's Study in Israel admissions page or the Masa Israel listing for Ohr Somayach Institutions.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Aish HaTorah – Aish Gesher
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A year-long post-high-school program in Jerusalem's Old City for young men graduating from public high schools who want a traditional yeshiva experience, studying Jewish texts at a serious level alongside leadership development, located in Aish's Old City campus with classrooms, a dining hall and gym.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Aish HaTorah's entry programs admissions page.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Aish HaTorah – Foundations Program
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A flexible-length program for college students on a gap year or semester break exploring Judaism with no background assumed, offering three hours of morning and three hours of afternoon classes covering Jewish philosophy, personal growth, history and text study, with optional evening programming.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Aish HaTorah's Foundations program admissions page.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Shapell's / Yeshiva Darché Noam
yeshivot_and_seminaries.organization: David Shapell College of Jewish Studies
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A yeshiva for English-speaking college graduates and young professionals in their 20s and 30s, founded in 1978, offering long-term (6+ months), medium-term (3-6 months) and short-term (1-3 months) Torah study tracks in Gemara, Chumash, Halacha and Hashkafa alongside character development.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Darche Noam's admissions page or the Masa Israel listing for Shapell's/Darche Noam.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Machon Shlomo
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A yeshiva founded in 1982 for Jewish, English-speaking college graduates and young professionals in their 20s and 30s pausing careers to spend a year or two on Torah learning, personal development and communal life, blending early-morning prayer, chevrusa study and lectures.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through Machon Shlomo's admissions page at machonshlomo.org.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Beit Chana Seminary (Tzfat) – Chul Program
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: One of the Lubavitch movement's most sought-after seminaries, historically for Israeli-Hebrew-speaking high school graduates training as future Chassidishe educators; its 'Chul program' extension accepts overseas students into a curriculum of personal and teaching skills alongside Limudei Kodesh, leading to a government-recognized 'Morot Lagolah' teaching certificate.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Beit Chana Tzfat Chul Program registration page; strong Hebrew proficiency is expected since most classes are taught in Hebrew.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Diaspora Yeshiva – Gap Year Program
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A gap-year program for male high school graduates on Mount Zion combining two to three daily sedarim of Torah/Talmud study tailored to each student's level with the option to earn an accredited Bachelor of Arts degree within a year, located minutes from the Western Wall.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.durationText: A year or less (including the optional B.A. track)
yeshivot_and_seminaries.cost: 5,600 NIS (~$1,800) per month for yeshiva tuition, room and board; approximately $12,000 additional for the B.A. track; tuition subsidies available on request
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply online at diasporayeshiva.com/application; applications are reviewed followed by an initial meeting with a rabbi.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Sha'arei Mevaseret Zion
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A post-high-school yeshiva for boys from North America, the UK, South America and Australia, offering Gemara shiurim across all learning levels alongside Mussar, Hashkafa, Chassidus, Tanach and Halacha study, with roughly 70% of students returning for an official Shana Bet second year.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.durationText: 1 year, with a common second-year (Shana Bet) option
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through ysmz.org's admissions page or the Masa Israel listing for Yeshivat Shaarei Mevaseret Zion.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Yeshivas Bircas HaTorah
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A yeshiva founded in 1989 just above the Cardo in the Old City's Jewish Quarter, offering full-time beginning, intermediate and advanced learning programs as well as intensive short-term programs for visitors from abroad, drawing students of all ages and Jewish backgrounds.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.durationText: Flexible: from several weeks up to a full year or more

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Midreshet AMIT
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: A women's midrasha built on Religious-Zionist ideals, combining Torah learning with hands-on community service — students volunteer with the children of Beit Hayeled, becoming part of the children's extended foster family alongside their studies.

yeshivot_and_seminaries.name: Michlalah Jerusalem College – Machal (Overseas Students Program)
yeshivot_and_seminaries.description: The Linda Pinsky School for Overseas Students (Machal) at Michlalah Jerusalem College, founded in 1964 to train Israeli teachers and expanded in 1969 to serve students from abroad; around 140 international students per year study Tanach, Halacha, Mussar and Machshava in a Torah-growth curriculum focused on personal commitment to Torah life.
yeshivot_and_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Machal @ Michlalah applications page.
